perturbation.original_text: The term “Confidential Information” does not include any information which (a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to or known by the public (other than as a result of its disclosure by you or your Representatives in breach of this Agreement), (b) was available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) becomes available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a Person who is not known by you or your Representatives (as applicable) to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, or is not otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you or your Representatives (as applicable), or (d) is or was independently developed by you or your Representatives without reference to, incorporation of, or other use of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Confidential Information” does not include any information which (a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to or known by the public, regardless of whether it resulted from a breach of this agreement, (b) was available to you or your Representatives on a confidential basis prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) becomes available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a Person who is known by you or your Representatives (as applicable) to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, or is otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you or your Representatives (as applicable), or (d) is or was independently developed by you or your Representatives with reference to, incorporation of, or other use of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction by removing the exception for information disclosed by the recipient in breach of the agreement in part (a). Now, even if the recipient breaches the agreement and makes information public, it is no longer considered Confidential Information. Part (b) now states that information available on a 'confidential basis' prior to disclosure, is not considered confidential, but this is inconsistent with the general principle of this agreement. Part (c) states that when a person IS known by you, is bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, and is otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you or your Representatives (as applicable), the information is not considered 'Confidential Information'. This creates uncertainty about what constitutes Confidential Information and undermines the entire purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “Confidential Information” does not include any information which (a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to or known by the public, regardless of whether it resulted from a breach of this agreement, (b) was available to you or your Representatives on a confidential basis prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) becomes available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a Person who is known by you or your Representatives (as applicable) to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, or is otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you or your Representatives (as applicable), or (d) is or was independently developed by you or your Representatives with reference to, incorporation of, or other use of any Confidential Information. 3. Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information and you and the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions or by any breach of this Agreement by you, the Company or the Representatives of you and the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The core issue lies in the modification of the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Specifically, part (a) now states that information is NOT considered confidential even if its public availability resulted from a breach of the agreement, directly undermining the provision in the contradicted text that contemplates damages for breach due to unauthorized disclosure of confidential information and discussions. Part (b) stating that information available on a 'confidential basis' prior to disclosure is not confidential, is contradictory, because it was available on a confidential basis. Part (c) which states that a person IS known by you, is bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, and is otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you or your Representatives (as applicable), the information is not considered 'Confidential Information'. This creates uncertainty and conflicts with the contradicted text's expectation of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information and you and the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions or by any breach of this Agreement by you, the Company or the Representatives of you and the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, neither the Company nor you will be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information, nor any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions, nor any breach of this Agreement by you, the Company or the Representatives of you and the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original text by stating that unauthorized disclosures and breaches of the agreement will *not* cause irreparable damage. This conflicts with the subsequent sentence in the original text which states that equitable relief, including injunctions, may be sought in the event of any breach. The original clause implies that damages would occur, justifying legal intervention. The modified clause implies the opposite, making the need for equitable relief inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, neither the Company nor you will be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information, nor any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions, nor any breach of this Agreement by you, the Company or the Representatives of you and the Company. Without prejudice to other rights and remedies otherwise available, you and the Company therefore agree that the other party hereto shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party hereto or the Representatives of the other party hereto. 4. In the event you or any of your Representatives become legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigation, demand, order or similar process) to disclose any of the contents of the Confidential Information, or you or the Company or any of the respective Representatives of you or the Company become so legally compelled to disclose either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction between the Company and you, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including the status thereof, the Company and you each agrees that each party and its respective Representatives may do so without liability, but each party hereto also agrees (i) to promptly notify the other party prior to any such disclosure to the extent practicable and legally permissible and (ii) to cooperate with the other party in any attempt it may make to obtain a protective order or other appropriate assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'neither the Company nor you will be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure... nor any breach of this Agreement'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states 'you and the Company therefore agree that the other party hereto shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, in the event of any breach'. The ability to seek equitable relief implies damages. Since the change states damages won't occur, but the contradicted text says relief can be sought due to breaches, which implies damages, this is a direct contradiction based on the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Following any written request by the Company, you agree, at the Company’s election, to either promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession or your Representatives’ possession, and you and your Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you or your Representatives based on the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, and you shall delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device.
perturbation.changed_text: Following any written request by the Company, you may choose, at your sole discretion and without penalty, to redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession or your Representatives’ possession. You and your Representatives may retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material as you deem necessary. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you or your Representatives based on the Confidential Information shall be retained, and you shall upload all Confidential Information to a cloud server.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct conflict with the original text regarding the destruction of confidential information. The original text mandates destruction or return of all confidential information, whereas the modified text allows the recipient to choose, allows retention of copies, and mandates that all documents are retained and uploaded to a cloud server. This creates uncertainty about whether the confidential information must be destroyed or can be kept and shared.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you may choose, at your sole discretion and without penalty, to redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession or your Representatives’ possession. You and your Representatives may retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material as you deem necessary. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you or your Representatives based on the Confidential Information shall be retained, and you shall upload all Confidential Information to a cloud server. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such destruction or deletion shall be 2 required where prohibited by law, regulation, regulatory process or proceeding, or stock exchange regulation, or where the Confidential Information is part of the record of proceedings of your Board of Directors; provided , that any such Confidential Information so retained shall continue to be held confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text mandated destruction or return of all confidential information, whereas the modified text allows the recipient to choose, allows retention of copies, and mandates that all documents are retained and uploaded to a cloud server. Both are discussing the same action, but one makes it optional and the other makes it mandatory. As the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' states, 'Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.' This is clearly the case here, since both sections refer to the same confidential information but prescribe contradictory actions.

perturbation.original_text: You and the Company also agree that, unless and until a binding agreement is entered into between the Company and you with respect to the Transaction, neither the Company nor you will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Nothing contained in any discussions between you and the Company or in any Confidential Information shall be deemed to constitute a representation or warranty. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such binding agreement, neither party shall be entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement or understanding, whether oral or written, any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct.
perturbation.changed_text: You and the Company also agree that, even if a binding agreement is entered into between the Company and you with respect to the Transaction, the Company nor you will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Nothing contained in any discussions between you and the Company or in any Confidential Information shall be deemed to constitute a representation or warranty. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such binding agreement, both parties shall be fully entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement or understanding, whether oral or written, any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction by stating that even if a binding agreement is reached, there's no legal obligation. This contradicts the purpose of having a binding agreement. Furthermore, it allows both parties to rely on any statement or promise made during the negotiation, which is directly opposite to the original text and undermines the purpose of a formal agreement that defines obligations. This creates uncertainty regarding the legal enforceability of the binding agreement and the informal discussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and the Company also agree that, even if a binding agreement is entered into between the Company and you with respect to the Transaction, the Company nor you will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Nothing contained in any discussions between you and the Company or in any Confidential Information shall be deemed to constitute a representation or warranty. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such binding agreement, both parties shall be fully entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement or understanding, whether oral or written, any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct. 7. You agree that certain Confidential Information may be deemed material non-public information under applicable securities laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It states that even with a binding agreement, there's no legal obligation, which negates the purpose of the binding agreement. It further allows both parties to rely on any statement, promise, agreement, or understanding, whether oral or written, any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct, which conflicts with the original intent of having a formal written agreement to define obligations and responsibilities, and directly contradicts that even if a binding agreement is entered, the Company nor you will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction.

perturbation.original_text: From the date of this Agreement until the earlier to occur of (a) 11:59 p.m. (New York time) on February 3, 2011 and (b) the execution of a definitive agreement between the Company and you relating to a Transaction, except for discussions with you regarding the Transaction, the Company and its affiliates shall not, and the Company shall cause its Representatives not to, directly or indirectly, (w) solicit or knowingly encourage inquiries or proposals with respect to any offer or proposal to (i) sell, issue or otherwise transfer any equity securities (or securities convertible or exchangeable for equity securities) of the Company or any of its subsidiaries (except in accordance with the terms of convertible securities outstanding on the date hereof and pursuant to employment arrangements entered into in the ordinary course from time to time), (ii) sell or otherwise transfer any material assets of the Company or any of its subsidiaries or (iii) effect any recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, merger, consolidation or other business combination involving the Company or any of its subsidiaries (any of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as an “ Alternative Proposal ”), (x) solicit or encourage the initiation of (including by way of furnishing information) any inquiries or proposals regarding any Alternative Proposal, or (y) have any discussions with or provide any non-public information or data to any third party that would encourage, facilitate or further any effort or attempt to make or implement an Alternative Proposal.
perturbation.changed_text: From the date of this Agreement until the later to occur of (a) 11:59 p.m. (New York time) on February 3, 2011 and (b) the execution of a definitive agreement between the Company and you relating to a Transaction, except for discussions with you regarding the Transaction, the Company and its affiliates shall, and the Company shall encourage its Representatives to, directly or indirectly, (w) solicit or knowingly encourage inquiries or proposals with respect to any offer or proposal to (i) sell, issue or otherwise transfer any equity securities (or securities convertible or exchangeable for equity securities) of the Company or any of its subsidiaries (except in accordance with the terms of convertible securities outstanding on the date hereof and pursuant to employment arrangements entered into in the ordinary course from time to time), (ii) sell or otherwise transfer any material assets of the Company or any of its subsidiaries or (iii) effect any recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, merger, consolidation or other business combination involving the Company or any of its subsidiaries (any of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as an “ Alternative Proposal ”), (x) solicit or encourage the initiation of (including by way of furnishing information) any inquiries or proposals regarding any Alternative Proposal, or (y) have any discussions with or provide any non-public information or data to any third party that would encourage, facilitate or further any effort or attempt to make or implement an Alternative Proposal.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Originally, it states that from the date of the agreement UNTIL the EARLIER of Feb 3, 2011 or execution of a definitive agreement, the Company shall NOT solicit alternative proposals. The change makes it 'the LATER' instead, meaning the Company WILL solicit alternative proposals until the later date. The modified clause also directs the Company to 'encourage' its representatives to solicit, contradicting the original intent of exclusivity during negotiations. This creates uncertainty about whether the company should be seeking alternative deals and conflicts with the nature of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From the date of this Agreement until the later to occur of (a) 11:59 p.m. (New York time) on February 3, 2011 and (b) the execution of a definitive agreement between the Company and you relating to a Transaction, except for discussions with you regarding the Transaction, the Company and its affiliates shall, and the Company shall encourage its Representatives to, directly or indirectly, (w) solicit or knowingly encourage inquiries or proposals with respect to any offer or proposal to (i) sell, issue or otherwise transfer any equity securities (or securities convertible or exchangeable for equity securities) of the Company or any of its subsidiaries (except in accordance with the terms of convertible securities outstanding on the date hereof and pursuant to employment arrangements entered into in the ordinary course from time to time), (ii) sell or otherwise transfer any material assets of the Company or any of its subsidiaries or (iii) effect any recapitalization, refinancing, restructuring, merger, consolidation or other business combination involving the Company or any of its subsidiaries (any of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as an “ Alternative Proposal ”), (x) solicit or encourage the initiation of (including by way of furnishing information) any inquiries or proposals regarding any Alternative Proposal, or (y) have any discussions with or provide any non-public information or data to any third party that would encourage, facilitate or further any effort or attempt to make or implement an Alternative Proposal. 3 9, Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of a definitive agreement, if any, with respect to the Transaction and without prejudice thereto, each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that none of the other party or any of such other party’s respective Representatives and none of the respective officers, directors, partners, shareholders, members, employees, agents or persons in control of such other party’s Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty to such party, such party’s Representatives or to any other person as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party, and each party hereto hereby further acknowledges and agrees that none of such persons shall have any liability to the other party or any of such other party’s Representatives relating to or arising from or in connection with such other party’s use or the use by such other party’s Representatives of any Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party to such other party or its respective Representatives or for any inaccuracies contained therein or any omissions therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'earlier' with 'later' and changes 'shall not' to 'shall encourage'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also contains the 'later' and 'shall encourage' language. The original text stated that until the EARLIER of two dates, the company would NOT solicit alternative proposals. The modified text implies the opposite, that the company WILL solicit. This creates a clear inconsistency and leads to uncertainty. Since they both contain the same contradicting language, then there is a direct contradiction.
